source: iConnections Global Alts 2025 — The Future of AI
summary: Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) event horizon has been pulled forward; 90% probability it is net positive for humanity
summary_extended: Baker argues that the timeline to Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) has been materially pulled forward, framing ASI as an 'event horizon' beyond which specific outcomes become unknowable but directionally transformative. He cites DeepSeek R1 and the emergence of new AI scaling laws as the key catalysts accelerating this timeline beyond prior expectations. Baker assigns a 90% probability that ASI will be net positive for humanity, expressing high conviction in a directional view rather than a specific stock call. The investment implication is that continued aggressive positioning in AI infrastructure and AI application companies remains warranted, as the probability-weighted outcome strongly favors those exposed to the AI buildout. No specific companies or price targets were cited; this is a macro-level framing thesis rather than a single-stock call.
detail: Baker frames ASI as an 'event horizon' beyond which outcomes are unknowable. DeepSeek R1 and the new scaling laws have accelerated the timeline to ASI. He assigns 90% probability to a positive outcome for humanity, implying continued aggressive investment in AI infrastructure and applications is warranted.
confidence_evidence: Baker uses probabilistic language ('90% chance') and frames this as a directional view rather than a specific investment call. Speculative by nature but stated with conviction.
quote_fragment: that event horizon has been pulled forward... 90% chance that's awesome for Humanity
